perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended except by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended by either (a) a writing signed by both parties hereto, or (b) verbal agreement confirmed by email from either party.
perturbation.explanation: This modifies the amendment process. The original clause states that an amendment *must* be in writing and signed by both parties. The modified version adds an alternative amendment path: 'verbal agreement confirmed by email from either party.' This is a clear contradiction, introducing ambiguity about the required form of an amendment. This introduces an In-Text contradiction in section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of California without reference to conflict of laws principles. This Agreement may be amended by either (a) a writing signed by both parties hereto, or (b) verbal agreement confirmed by email from either party. 13. Each party acknowledges that its breach of the Agreement will cause irreparable damage and hereby agrees that the other party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement, as well as such further relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction. Additionally, in the event of a breach by the Recipient, the disclosing party shall be entitled to recover the costs of enforcing this Agreement including, without limitation reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments to the agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text introduces an alternative method for amending the agreement: a verbal agreement confirmed by email from either party. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, as the modification was applied directly within the contradicted section. The change clearly contradicts the original requirement of a written agreement signed by both parties, introducing a structural flaw and ambiguity regarding the valid methods for amending the agreement, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party, unless such information is deemed necessary for the completion of a transaction between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'unless such information is deemed necessary for the completion of a transaction between the parties' this creates a contradiction. The original text establishes a clear prohibition against violating third-party proprietary rights. The added exception now allows for such violation if deemed 'necessary'. This introduces uncertainty and makes it difficult to determine when a party is allowed to violate third-party rights, creating an In-Text contradiction in section 15.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party, unless such information is deemed necessary for the completion of a transaction between the parties. 16. Neither party will assign or transfer any rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original text's absolute prohibition on violating third-party proprietary rights. The added phrase 'unless such information is deemed necessary for the completion of a transaction between the parties' directly contradicts the original prohibition. This creates a situation where one section forbids a certain action, while the changed section permits it under specific conditions, leading to a clear in-text contradiction within section 15 as mentioned in the explanation, and as highlighted between the Original Text and Contradicted Text being the same.
